en_body: From the morning I awaken still bewildered in the after-sound of the final dream, I place my two feet upon the earth, and until I climb into bed to sleep, my two feet carry me to and fro through the whole day. This heel steps forward then the other heel follows...becoming a journey whether inside the house or out along the lane. The earth stands firm amid the city belonging to a Time-Space that has indulged my sentimental, cowardly spirit. Those longings for travel when I first came of age: craving to journey to Australia, to Africa...since settling in the mighty United States as my second homeland: staying on and on, I no longer feel excited by the dream of travel, although most people in the Third World countries dream the illusory dream of forests of money, seas of silver, adorning America as a material paradise. Like the golden phoenix from the jeweled paradise of royal imagination sometimes falling into Nature's realm of leaves and clear streams, slowly becoming simple, its gold-and-silver feathers turning fresh into tender down: expectations fixed upon the urgent needs of food and clothing, my two feet wander round and round one quiet corner of the city and the Consciousness realm demanding complex contemplation of human relationships. Awareness dives deep into the subconscious realm, forgetting entirely the direction of my two feet: suddenly this morning my two feet call to me: "Have you yet found the direction for the footsteps going forward?" Wherever one goes, a time must come to return: destiny at most grants fewer than "thirty-six thousand days." Half a lifetime passed before I became conscious of the necessity of my own steps upon the face of this blue planet overflowing with rights and wrongs, although the boundless Universe still kindles its towering source of fire without needing anyone to know. Thank you, my two feet, still steadfastly walking.
note: “Thirty-six thousand days” is a traditional reckoning of a human lifetime of roughly one hundred years.
